title: Platform _defaults.yaml — top-level key guard (#658 fast-follow)
description: MINIMAL schema for conf.d _defaults.yaml. The top-level keys are STRICT (additionalProperties:false), so a typo like `state_flters` / `defalts` / `_routing_defualts`-class mistakes that would otherwise SILENTLY drop the whole platform-default block (the highest-blast-radius config file — affects every tenant under that directory) fail at author/CI time. NESTED values are intentionally LEFT LOOSE ({} = accept anything): the keys under `defaults` (metric names) and `state_filters` (filter names) are dynamic and platform-authored, so they are NOT modelled here (a full structural schema is the deferred #658 item (a)). This is the cheap, high-value middle ground between 'no validation' and 'full JSON Schema'.
$comment: The `_*` properties mirror _lib_constants.py VALID_RESERVED_KEYS (the Go/Python/Schema 3-way reserved-key SSOT). tests/lint/test_check_confd_schema.py asserts this set stays a superset of VALID_RESERVED_KEYS so a newly added reserved key can't silently make this guard false-reject a legitimate inherited override in _defaults.yaml. The `^_state_` / `^_routing` patternProperties mirror VALID_RESERVED_PREFIXES (prefix-class keys are accepted, exactly as the tenant validator does — prefix-internal typos are out of scope here, same as the tenant model).

defaults.title: Platform default thresholds (metric → value)
defaults.description: Map of metric key → default threshold (scalar or scheduled). Keys are dynamic metric names → values left loose; only the top-level `defaults` key itself is guarded.

state_filters.title: Platform default state filters
state_filters.description: Map of filter-name → {reasons, severity, default_state}. Filter names are dynamic → values left loose.

_routing_defaults.title: Default routing inherited by all tenants under this directory
